title: Live backfill would flip status planned->complete (FALSE completion); deriveStatus treats scaffold implementation-summary.md presence as completion with no checklist gate
claim: A live refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder against phase 009 would WRITE derived.status:'complete' (reproduced via read-only derive+merge+serialize probe against the real folder). Phase 009 is BUILDABLE-BUT-UNAUTHORED scaffold (completion_pct:0, D3); correct value already in legacy file is 'planned'. deriveStatus (graph-metadata-parser.ts:1201-1218): scaffold frontmatter statuses are bracketed placeholders -> normalizeDerivedStatus null for all ranked docs; implementation-summary.md EXISTS (verbatim scaffold) -> implementationSummaryDoc truthy; NO checklist.md (Level 1) -> !checklistDoc -> return {status:'complete'}. existing 'planned' only consulted in the lean-parent (!implementationSummaryDoc) branch 009 does not take. Naive 'node backfill-graph-metadata.js --spec-folder <009>' is the unsafe form; statusOverride:'planned' (1505-1508->1173-1176) mitigates.
evidenceRefs: graph-metadata-parser.ts:1186-1192 (all ranked frontmatter statuses null for scaffold)
counterevidenceSought: statusOverride or lean-parent branch salvage it? refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder passes no statusOverride by default (1505-1508); lean-parent existingStatus preservation (1207-1212) only when impl-summary ABSENT -- 009 has it. No code path yields 'planned' under default invocation. Reproduced empirically.
alternativeExplanation: only if 'scaffold-with-impl-summary = structurally complete even if content-empty' is intended -- contradicts completion_pct:0 and D3, makes metadata useless for authored-vs-scaffold
scope: false completion signal propagating into graph/memory index; metadata accuracy, no data loss
downgradeTrigger: downgrade P2 if all backfill entrypoints targeting scaffold phases documented to require statusOverride OR deriveStatus patched to consult completion_pct/open-tasks
upgradeTrigger: upgrade P0 if repo-wide sweep (iter 5) finds false-complete mislabeling many genuinely-incomplete phases -> systemic tooling defect

title: Live backfill regresses derived synopsis/tier fields vs legacy because source docs are unauthored scaffolds (importance important->normal; causal_summary -> spec.md placeholder)
claim: Beyond status, (a) importance_tier flips important->normal (deriveImportanceTier 1234-1242 reads decision-record/plan/spec/impl-summary frontmatter; scaffold supplies 'normal', legacy 'important' not preserved; fallback 'important' not reached proving a scaffold doc carries importance_tier:normal); (b) causal_summary becomes literal spec.md placeholder '[What is broken, missing, or inefficient?...]' via deriveCausalSummary/derivePacketSynopsis over scaffold body -- worse than legacy 'Phase 1: speckit-command-goal-prompt-offer'. No data loss but LESS accurate than legacy text it replaces.
evidenceRefs: graph-metadata-parser.ts:1234-1242 (deriveImportanceTier fallback 'important' not reached -> scaffold supplies 'normal')
counterevidenceSought: is 'normal' from real frontmatter or selectFirstValue fallback? fallback is 'important' yet output 'normal' -> a scaffold frontmatter carries importance_tier:normal (INFERRED-partial: exact source doc not re-opened this iteration; value empirically certain)
alternativeExplanation: deriveImportanceTier/deriveCausalSummary working as designed -- faithfully reflect unauthored source docs; regression is symptom of D3 (scaffold sources) not independent tooling bug
scope: accuracy regression in low-stakes derived fields; self-corrects on spec.md authoring (D3)
downgradeTrigger: informational when spec.md authored on pickup

claim: seeded: a live backfill would 'regenerate from current docs rather than erroring out' (benign)
evidence: probe reproduced compute path against real folder: legacy-tolerant load succeeded; derive+merge succeeded; would-write a full schema-conformant JSON matching sibling 010 shape
BUT outcome is NOT benign: status regresses planned->complete (FALSE, D4-P1-001), importance important->normal + synopsis->placeholder (D4-P2-001)
seeded dry-run counters {refreshed:1,failed:[]} only proved no-write in dry-run, not the live content
bearing: seeded narrative undersold the regression; adversarial code-trace + empirical derive surfaced D4-P1-001

claim: graph-metadata repair touches ONLY graph-metadata.json; derive reads canonical docs but never writes them
evidence: writeGraphMetadataFile:1467 canClassifyAsGraphMetadataPath guard refuses non-graph-metadata dest
deriveGraphMetadata:1258 collectPacketDocs reads CANONICAL_PACKET_DOCS (51-62) read-only; no write calls in derive path
handover.md (sole authored artifact, D3) read for synopsis/key-file/entity derivation, left byte-identical

claim: description.json is independent and at no risk from a graph-metadata-only repair
evidence: separate generator (generate-context.js); already valid JSON (read, 25 lines)

reasoning: Write target is graph-metadata.json ONLY (filePath 1503, canClassifyAsGraphMetadataPath guard 1467); derive reads canonical docs but never writes; handover.md (sole authored content) read-only; description.json separate code path already valid JSON. So repair preserves all authored content and is orthogonal to D2 ownership. BUT default live invocation injects status:'complete' (FALSE -- deriveStatus 1201-1218 treats scaffold impl-summary presence as complete with no checklist gate), downgrades importance important->normal, replaces synopsis with spec.md placeholder. Repair unblocks validate.sh FILE_UNPARSEABLE (D1) but MUST NOT be read as truthful status.
greenlightRecommendation: GREENLIGHT independent of D2 on parseability/content axes; mandatory caveat: regenerated status will read 'complete' (false) unless statusOverride:'planned' supplied OR accepted as known transient pending spec.md authoring (D3)

note: All 4 dimensions (D1-D4) now have first-pass genuine coverage. Repair is safe to greenlight vs authored content and the D2 ownership question (orthogonal). Single caveat: false-complete status regression (D4-P1-001) -- mitigate with statusOverride or accept as transient. stop_policy=max-iterations -> broaden not converge. Iteration 5 recommended angle: test generality of D4-P1-001 repo-wide (deriveStatus false-complete heuristic may mislabel many unauthored-scaffold phases) -- could upgrade to systemic P0. Secondary: re-challenge D2 verdict; re-confirm D1 1/2425 sweep.
